titles.ar: بَابُ أَنَّ الْأَئِمَّةَ ( عليهم السلام ) لَوْ سُتِرَ عَلَيْهِمْ لَأَخْبَرُوا كُلَّ امْرِئٍ بِمَا لَهُ وَ عَلَيْهِ
titles.en: Chapter 51- The Imamsasws, if secrets would have been kept, theyasws would have informed every person with whatever was for him and against him

titles.ar: بَابُ أَنَّ الْأَئِمَّةَ ( عليهم السلام ) لَوْ سُتِرَ عَلَيْهِمْ لَأَخْبَرُوا كُلَّ امْرِئٍ بِمَا لَهُ وَ عَلَيْهِ
titles.en: Chapter 51- The Imamsasws, if secrets would have been kept, theyasws would have informed every person with whatever was for him and against him

segments: قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) لَوْ كَانَ لِأَلْسِنَتِكُمْ أَوْكِيَةٌ لَحَدَّثْتُ كُلَّ امْرِئٍ بِمَا لَهُ وَ عَلَيْهِ .
arabic.text: قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) لَوْ كَانَ لِأَلْسِنَتِكُمْ أَوْكِيَةٌ لَحَدَّثْتُ كُلَّ امْرِئٍ بِمَا لَهُ وَ عَلَيْهِ .

segments: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Fazalat Bin Ayoub, from Aban Bin Usman, from Abdul Wahid Bin Al Mukhrat who said,
‘Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If there was a stopper for your tongues (to restrain them), I<sup>asws</sup> would have narrated every person with what is for him and against him’.
en.hubeali.text:
A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Fazalat Bin Ayoub, from Aban Bin Usman, from Abdul Wahid Bin Al Mukhrat who said,

‘Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If there was a stopper for your tongues (to restrain them), I<sup>asws</sup> would have narrated every person with what is for him and against him’.

segments: 1. A number of our people has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Fadala ibn Ayyub from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from ‘Abd al-Wahid ibn al-Mukhtar who has that abu Ja‘far (a.s.) has said the following. “Had there been a thing (zip) to contain (to sack) your tongues I would have spoken to everyone about what is for or against him.”
en.sarwar.text: 1. A number of our people has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Fadala ibn Ayyub from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from ‘Abd al-Wahid ibn al-Mukhtar who has that abu Ja‘far (a.s.) has said the following. “Had there been a thing (zip) to contain (to sack) your tongues I would have spoken to everyone about what is for or against him.”

gradings.label: مجهول
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/139)

gradings.label: ضعيف
gradings.text: Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: ضعيف - Sahih al-Kafi

narratorChain.raw: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ فَضَالَةَ بْنِ أَيُّوبَ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ عَبْدِ الْوَاحِدِ بْنِ الْمُخْتَارِ قَالَ

titles.ar: بَابُ أَنَّ الْأَئِمَّةَ ( عليهم السلام ) لَوْ سُتِرَ عَلَيْهِمْ لَأَخْبَرُوا كُلَّ امْرِئٍ بِمَا لَهُ وَ عَلَيْهِ
titles.en: Chapter 51- The Imamsasws, if secrets would have been kept, theyasws would have informed every person with whatever was for him and against him

segments: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا بَصِيرٍ يَقُولُ قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مِنْ أَيْنَ أَصَابَ أَصْحَابَ عَلِيٍّ مَا أَصَابَهُمْ مَعَ عِلْمِهِمْ بِمَنَايَاهُمْ وَ بَلَايَاهُمْ قَالَ فَأَجَابَنِي شِبْهَ الْمُغْضَبِ مِمَّنْ ذَلِكَ إِلَّا مِنْهُمْ فَقُلْتُ مَا يَمْنَعُكَ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ قَالَ ذَلِكَ بَابٌ أُغْلِقَ إِلَّا أَنَّ الْحُسَيْنَ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ صَلَوَاتٌ عَلَيْهِمَا فَتَحَ مِنْهُ شَيْئاً يَسِيراً
ثُمَّ قَالَ يَا أَبَا مُحَمَّدٍ إِنَّ أُولَئِكَ كَانَتْ عَلَى أَفْوَاهِهِمْ أَوْكِيَةٌ .
arabic.text:
سَمِعْتُ أَبَا بَصِيرٍ يَقُولُ قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مِنْ أَيْنَ أَصَابَ أَصْحَابَ عَلِيٍّ مَا أَصَابَهُمْ مَعَ عِلْمِهِمْ بِمَنَايَاهُمْ وَ بَلَايَاهُمْ قَالَ فَأَجَابَنِي شِبْهَ الْمُغْضَبِ مِمَّنْ ذَلِكَ إِلَّا مِنْهُمْ فَقُلْتُ مَا يَمْنَعُكَ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ قَالَ ذَلِكَ بَابٌ أُغْلِقَ إِلَّا أَنَّ الْحُسَيْنَ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ صَلَوَاتٌ عَلَيْهِمَا فَتَحَ مِنْهُ شَيْئاً يَسِيراً

ثُمَّ قَالَ يَا أَبَا مُحَمَّدٍ إِنَّ أُولَئِكَ كَانَتْ عَلَى أَفْوَاهِهِمْ أَوْكِيَةٌ .

segments: And by this chain, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Sinan, from Abdullah Bin Muskan who said,

‘I heard Abu Baeer saying, ‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘From where did the companions of Ali<sup>asws</sup> attain (the knowledge of) what is going to hit them, along with the knowledge of their deaths and their calamities?’ He (Abu Baseer) said, ‘So he<sup>asws</sup> answered me resembling the anger: ‘From whom would that be except for themselves?’ So I said, ‘So what is preventing you<sup>asws</sup>, may I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>?’ (i.e., to foretell our future to us). He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘That is a locked door, except that Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> Bin Ali<sup>asws</sup> opened something small from it’.
Then he<sup>asws</sup> said:’O Abu Muhammad! Those ones, they used to have stoppers upon their mouths’.
en.hubeali.text:
And by this chain, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Sinan, from Abdullah Bin Muskan who said,

‘I heard Abu Baeer saying, ‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘From where did the companions of Ali<sup>asws</sup> attain (the knowledge of) what is going to hit them, along with the knowledge of their deaths and their calamities?’ He (Abu Baseer) said, ‘So he<sup>asws</sup> answered me resembling the anger: ‘From whom would that be except for themselves?’ So I said, ‘So what is preventing you<sup>asws</sup>, may I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>?’ (i.e., to foretell our future to us). He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘That is a locked door, except that Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> Bin Ali<sup>asws</sup> opened something small from it’.

Then he<sup>asws</sup> said:’O Abu Muhammad! Those ones, they used to have stoppers upon their mouths’.

segments: 2. Through the same chain of narrators it is narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Sinan from ‘Abdallah ibn Muskan who has said that he heard abu Basir say that he said to abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) the following. “What made the supporters of Ali (a.s.) suffer the troubles despite the fact that they knew about their deaths and (causes of their) suffering?” the Imam (a.s.) replied me in a way as if he seemed angry, “from who could it come except from their own selves.?” I then asked, “What prevents you, may Allah take my soul in service for your cause, from telling it all (to your friends). The Imam (a.s.) said, “It is a door that is closed door except that al-Husayn ibn Ali (a.s.) opened it to a small degree (told a few things about future to his supporters just before their martyrdom).” The Imam (a.s.) then said, “O abu Muhammad, those people had a tie (zip) over their mouths.”
en.sarwar.text: 2. Through the same chain of narrators it is narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Sinan from ‘Abdallah ibn Muskan who has said that he heard abu Basir say that he said to abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) the following. “What made the supporters of Ali (a.s.) suffer the troubles despite the fact that they knew about their deaths and (causes of their) suffering?” the Imam (a.s.) replied me in a way as if he seemed angry, “from who could it come except from their own selves.?” I then asked, “What prevents you, may Allah take my soul in service for your cause, from telling it all (to your friends). The Imam (a.s.) said, “It is a door that is closed door except that al-Husayn ibn Ali (a.s.) opened it to a small degree (told a few things about future to his supporters just before their martyrdom).” The Imam (a.s.) then said, “O abu Muhammad, those people had a tie (zip) over their mouths.”

gradings.text: Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: ضعيف - Sahih al-Kafi

narratorChain.raw: وَبِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُسْكَانَ قَالَ:
